title: Today's AI Brief: I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking
summary: A look at the wild world of AI-powered products, from a crypto-cannabis vape to Warp's bet on open-source development with GPT-5.5.
imageAlt: Image from theverge.com for I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking
imageCaption: I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking (theverge.com)

body.text: I'm Leo Stone, and I'm here to walk you through some of the latest AI news. I have to say, today's stories are quite something. Let's dive in.

First up, I came across an article on The Verge about an AI-powered cannabis vape that's supposed to give you Bitcoin for smoking. Yes, you read that right. The product, called Gudtrip, seems like a wild combination of crypto, AI, and cannabis. I'm not sure if it's real or just a joke, but it's definitely... interesting. I mean, who wouldn't want to get paid in Bitcoin for, you know, taking a puff?

Next, I looked at Warp's announcement about using GPT-5.5 and OpenAI models to coordinate coding agents across different development workflows. This one caught my attention because it's a big bet on open-source development. Warp is trying to make coding more collaborative and efficient, and it's using AI to do it. The idea of AI helping developers work together seamlessly is pretty exciting.

And, there's another story worth mentioning - the increasing use of AI in unexpected areas. It seems like AI is being applied to everything from cannabis vapes to coding tools. It's making me wonder what other surprising areas AI will pop up in next.

I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: AI I went looking for the AI weed vape that gives you Bitcoin for smoking Gudtrip is the most ridiculous AI/crypto/weed product to ever touch the internet. Could it possibly be real? by Robert Hart May 31, 2026, 1:00 PM UTC Image: The Verge, Gudtrip, Getty Images Robert Hart is a London-based reporter at The Verge covering all things AI and a Senior Tarbell... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Warp’s big bet on building open source with GPT-5.5 is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Warp uses GPT-5.5 and OpenAI models to coordinate coding agents across local, cloud, and open-source development workflows. The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
